train_3968.llm_answer: Consumer advocate: there is no doubt that the government is responsible for the increased cost of gasoline, because the government's policies have significantly increased consumer demand for fuel, and as a result of increasing demand, the price of gasoline has risen steadily, regardless of other factors.

train_316.llm_answer: Statement1: To suit the needs of corporate clients, advertising agencies have successfully modified a strategy originally developed for political campaigns. train_4399.llm_answer: Statement1: Consumer magazine: because front-loading washers use less water than top-loading washers, ordinary powder detergent does not dissolve readily in front-loading washers. train_2130.llm_answer: Statement1: The dean of computing must be respected by the academic staff and be competent to oversee the use of computers on campus. train_1423.llm_answer: Statement1: Consumer advocate: ephedra is a naturally occurring compound that some people use as a weight-loss aid. train_594.llm_answer: Statement1: Often patients with ankle fractures that are stable, and thus do not require surgery, are given follow-up x-rays because their orthopedists are concerned about possibly having misjudged the stability of the fracture. Statement2: When a number of follow-up x-rays were reviewed, however, all the fractures that had initially been judged stable were found to have healed correctly. Statement3: Therefore, it is a waste of money to order follow-up x-rays of ankle fractures initially judged stable, especially since general practitioners are less reliable in assessing stability than orthopedists.
train_594.llm_explanation: The modification emphasizes that the reliability of orthopedists in judging stability is higher than that of general practitioners, which supports the argument that follow-up x-rays are unnecessary for stable fractures. train_3979.llm_answer: Statement1: More and more law firms specializing in corporate taxes are paid on an hourly rate basis. Statement2: Under this arrangement, if a case is won, the firm usually receives less than it would have received if it had been paid on a contingency-fee basis. Statement3: Most firms are likely to make less under the hourly rate arrangement.
train_3979.llm_explanation: By changing the payment structure to hourly rates and indicating that firms earn less under this arrangement, Premise 2, which states that firms take in fewer cases under contingency-fee arrangements, supports the argument that firms are likely to earn less overall. train_4104.llm_answer: Statement1: It is popularly believed that a poem has whatever meaning is assigned to it by the reader. train_4104.llm_explanation: The modification of Statement 2 clarifies that the discussion of aesthetic value hinges on the agreement of two readers, making Premise 2 a necessary assumption for the argument. train_1182.llm_answer: Critic : rock music is musically bankrupt and socially destructive, but at least the album covers of rock lps from the 1960s and 1970s often featured innovative visual art. train_2941.llm_answer: Statement1: Music critic: fewer and fewer musicians are studying classical music, decreasing the likelihood that those with real aptitude for such music will be performing it. train_939.llm_answer: Statement1: Twelve years ago and again five years ago, there were extended periods when the darfir republic's currency, the pundra, was weak: its value was unusually low relative to the world's most stable currencies. Both times a weak pundra made darfir's manufactured products a bargain on world markets, and darfir's exports were up substantially. Statement2: Now some politicians are saying that, in order to cause another similarly sized increase in exports, the government should allow the pundra to become weak again, despite the fact that a sharp improvement in the efficiency of darfir's manufacturing plants would make darfir's products a bargain on world markets even without any weakening of the pundra relative to other currencies.
train_939.llm_explanation: The modification to Statement 2 introduces the idea that improved efficiency could achieve the same export increase without needing a weak pundra, which directly undermines the argument for allowing the currency to weaken. train_1248.llm_answer: Statement1: Educator: some experimental educational programs, based on the principle that children's first education should take place at home, instruct parents in how to be their child's "first teacher." train_4022.llm_answer: Statement1: Super express shipping company has implemented a new distribution system that can get almost every package to its destination the day after it is sent. train_2437.llm_answer: The photographs that the store developed were quite unsatisfactory. The customer claims to have handled the film incorrectly. Neither the film nor the camera was defective. train_3914.llm_answer: Statement1: The miscarriage of justice in the barker case was due to the mistaken views held by some of the forensic scientists involved in the case, who believed that they owed allegiance only to the prosecuting lawyers. train_4467.llm_answer: Statement1: Naturalist : sent2: the recent claims that the tasmanian tiger is not extinct are false. train_168.llm_answer: Statement1: If one wants to succeed, then one should act as though one were genuinely confident about one's abilities, even if one actually possesses genuine self-doubt. train_2814.llm_answer: Statement1: A company has developed a new sensing device that, according to the company's claims, detects weak, ultralow-frequency electromagnetic signals associated with a beating heart. train_2710.llm_answer: Statement1: Virginia and her brother William disagree over when their father was born: Virginia claims it was in 1935 and William claims it was in 1933. train_3939.llm_answer: Statement1: Human skin gives off an array of gaseous substances, including carbon dioxide and lactic acid, both of which attract mosquitoes. train_1851.llm_answer: Economist: in today's post-industrial economy, higher education is essential for achieving access to high-paying jobs. train_1170.llm_answer: Companies wishing to boost sales of merchandise should use in-store displays to catch customers' attention. train_97.llm_answer: Art critic: nowadays, museum visitors seldom pause to look at a work of art for even a full minute. They look, perhaps take a snapshot, and move on. train_2815.llm_answer: Statement1: Critic: people today place an especially high value on respect for others; yet, in their comedy acts, many of today's most popular comedians display blatant disrespect for others. train_838.llm_answer: Margaret came home after a long day of work to find her front door open and her waste basket knocked over with all of the contents strewn about the kitchen floor.
